title: Marked Letters (Methylation and Gene Expression On/Off)
learning_goal: Learn that bacteria can chemically 'tag' specific short DNA motifs with a methyl group (methylation), without changing the underlying nucleotide letter, and that in Campylobacter jejuni a single gene (cj0031) can itself phase-vary ON/OFF, switching genome-wide methylation - and downstream gene expression - on and off as a group ('phasevarion').
simplification_note: This level uses a REAL, published Campylobacter jejuni system: the phase-variable Type IIG restriction-modification enzyme Cj0031 (also called ModH) in strain NCTC11168, which methylates adenine within 5'-CCYGA (Y = C or T) sites genome-wide (Anjum et al., Nucleic Acids Research 2016, PMC4889913). Two things are simplified for teaching: (1) cj0031 itself switches ON/OFF via slipped-strand mispairing in a homopolymeric tract - real, but we present it here as a simple toggle rather than modeling the repeat-tract mechanism itself; and (2) the actual paper found only a WEAK statistical association between CCYGA methylation and measured gene expression changes (not a clean single-gene on/off switch) - so this level is honest that 'motif present' does not automatically mean 'this exact gene is regulated by it', especially when the motif sits deep inside a coding sequence rather than near a promoter or ribosome binding site.
rules: This level has 2 rounds: Round 1 checks jlpA, cadF and ciaB for the real Campylobacter methylation motif; Round 2 explores the real cj0031 phase-variation mechanism using a literature-documented example gene (peb1A).
Methylation never changes a nucleotide letter — the DNA sequence and protein stay identical either way.
A blob (🔵) means that base is really methylated. No blob means the motif isn't present in that fragment - use the motif table to understand why.
You must give an ON/OFF answer for all 3 genes in Round 1 to unlock Round 2.
In Round 2, the SAME gene's methylation depends on whether the cj0031 enzyme is phase-ON or phase-OFF - read the explanation before answering the final question.

motif_table.description: Material for Round 1: the real Campylobacter jejuni methylation motif recognized by the phase-variable Cj0031 (ModH) enzyme (strain NCTC11168), and the simplified on/off rule used in this game.

motifs.motif: CCYGA (Y = C or T, i.e. CCCGA or CCTGA)
motifs.methylated_base: the final A becomes N6-methyladenine (6mA), on one strand only (hemimethylated)
motifs.methyltransferase_family: Cj0031 (ModH) - a phase-variable Type IIG restriction-modification enzyme unique to C. jejuni
motifs.game_rule: If this motif is found within the gene fragment shown AND cj0031 is phase-ON, that occurrence gets methylated.

motif_table.default_rule: If the motif is absent from the fragment, there is nothing for Cj0031 to methylate there, regardless of its phase state.

materials_provided: Each gene's real sequence, already assembled, with a methylation blob (🔵) marking any base that's actually methylated
instructions: 1. Look at each gene's real sequence and the motif table. If a blob (🔵) sits on the sequence, that base is really methylated.
2. Decide: is this gene's expression ON or OFF? (For these 3 genes, the motif isn't present in the fragment shown - so methylation can't act here at all.)
3. Check your answer. The game reveals the correct call and explains why.
4. Repeat for all 3 genes to unlock Round 2.
5. Meet peb1A - a real, literature-documented gene that DOES contain a genuine CCTGA site, deep inside its coding sequence.
6. Toggle cj0031 phase ON/OFF to see whether that real site actually gets methylated, and read why 'methylated' isn't automatically the same as 'this gene is switched off'.
7. Answer the final question about what the real published research actually found.

jlpA.reasoning: CCYGA (CCCGA or CCTGA) does not appear anywhere in this fragment, so Cj0031 has nothing to methylate here - expression stays ON regardless of cj0031's phase state.

cadF.reasoning: CCYGA (CCCGA or CCTGA) does not appear anywhere in this fragment, so Cj0031 has nothing to methylate here - expression stays ON regardless of cj0031's phase state.

ciaB.reasoning: CCYGA (CCCGA or CCTGA) does not appear anywhere in this fragment, so Cj0031 has nothing to methylate here - expression stays ON regardless of cj0031's phase state.

round2.title: Round 2 - The Real cj0031 Phasevarion (using peb1A, a real example)
round2.gene_note: peb1A (locus cj0921c) is a real C. jejuni adhesin gene. It's one of the genes discussed in the actual Cj0031 phasevarion study (Anjum et al. 2016) as showing an expression difference between the cj0031-ON and cj0031-deletion strains.

real_motif_context.position_note: This CCTGA is real GenBank/genome sequence from inside peb1A, about 538 nt (roughly codon 180) into the gene - deep in the coding region, not near the promoter or ribosome binding site.

round2.intro: peb1A's DNA contains a real CCTGA site, all the time - that sequence never changes. Whether it actually gets methylated depends on whether the Cj0031 (ModH) enzyme is in its phase-ON or phase-OFF state, which C. jejuni switches stochastically via slipped-strand mispairing in a repeat tract within the cj0031 gene itself.

environments.label: cj0031 phase ON
environments.explanation: When cj0031 is phase-ON, the ModH enzyme is made and active. The real CCTGA site in peb1A gets methylated (6mA on one strand).

environments.label: cj0031 phase OFF
environments.explanation: When cj0031 is phase-OFF (the gene is switched off by slipped-strand mispairing), no ModH enzyme is made. The CCTGA site in peb1A is still physically present but stays unmethylated.

round2.unaffected_genes_note: jlpA and ciaB don't contain the CCYGA motif at all in the fragments you saw, so toggling cj0031 never affects them - only genes that actually carry a real CCYGA site (like this peb1A example) can be touched by this mechanism.
round2.quiz_question: The actual published study (Anjum et al. 2016) compared a cj0031-ON strain to a cj0031-deletion strain and looked at whether CCYGA methylation predicted which genes changed expression. What did they actually find?

quiz_options.label: Only a weak statistical association - many genes changed expression, but it correlated poorly with which genes actually had CCYGA sites
A perfect one-to-one switch - every gene with a CCYGA site changed expression in lockstep with methylation
No effect at all - gene expression was identical between the two strains

round2.quiz_explanation: The real paper found hundreds of genes changed expression between the cj0031-ON and deletion strains, but only a WEAK association with actual CCYGA sites - the authors explicitly note this. That's honest, real biology: just because a gene is methylated somewhere in its sequence doesn't mean that site is the reason its expression changed. Regulatory effects are most reliably linked to sites near promoters or ribosome-binding regions, not just anywhere the motif happens to occur - and most real motif copies (like the ones methylated across the genome) exist for genome defense, not gene regulation.

discussion_prompt: Ask players: unlike levels 2-4, methylation doesn't change any nucleotide LETTER at all - the DNA sequence and the protein it would make are identical whether cj0031 is phase-ON or phase-OFF. So what actually changed between Round 1 and Round 2? (Answer: nothing in the DNA sequence changed - what changed was whether the METHYLATION MACHINERY (Cj0031/ModH) was active. This is 'epigenetics' - control layered on top of the DNA sequence. And real research shows it's messier than a clean on/off switch: having the motif doesn't guarantee a gene's expression is controlled by it - that link was only weakly supported even in the actual published study.)
